data.title: HP Enterprise rises as Deutsche Bank upgrades amid 'undemanding' valuation
data.description:
• HP Enterprise (HPE) was in focus on Thursday as Deutsche Bank upgraded the IT giant, citing its "undemanding" valuation.
  • Shares rose 2.3% in premarket trading.
  • "HPE's valuation appears relatively undemanding, with shares trading at 5x CY2026E EV/EBITDA and 9x EPS (generally lagging Hardware peers, and well below Networking comps)," analyst Matt Niknam wrote in a note to clients. "Even when using pro forma CY26E estimates (including Juniper, and with Networking making up 40%+ of operating profit), we estimate shares trade at 6x CY26E EBITDA and 8x EPS, well below peers." Niknam raised his rating on HP Enterprise to Buy from Hold and put a $25 price target on the stock.
  • Niknam also noted that the pending acquisition of Juniper Networks (NYSE:JNPR), which is slated to close early next year, pending regulatory approval, could give HP Enterprise "newfound scale" in networking, along with "sizable" cost synergies.
  • "We estimate the deal should deliver low/mid-single digit accretion (EPS and FCF/share) in Year 1, ramping to the double-digits in Year 2," Niknam added.
  • In addition, Niknam praised the company's standalone business, as it is seeing "positive inflections" in its core segments, thanks to artificial intelligence and traditional server sales, along with hybrid cloud and its own networking businesses.

data.title: U.S. corporate profits falls 0.4% in Q3
data.description:
Q3 U.S. corporate profits were -0.4% Q/Q vs. +3.6% in Q2, the U.S. Bureau of Economic Analysis said on Thursday. On a Y/Y basis, Q3 corporate profits increased 5.9%.

Profits from current production (corporate profits with inventory valuation and capital consumption adjustments) decreased $15.0 billion in the third quarter, a downward revision of $4.9 billion from the previous estimate.

Profits of domestic financial corporations increased $3.0 billion in the third quarter, an upward revision of $5.6 billion from the previous estimate.

Profits of domestic nonfinancial corporations increased $24.9 billion, a downward revision of $5.9 billion. Rest-of-the-world profits decreased $42.9 billion, a downward revision of $4.6 billion. Receipts decreased $61.5 billion, and payments decreased $18.7 billion.

data.title: BigBear.ai announces exchange of portion of existing 6.00% convertible senior notes due 2026
data.description:
• BigBear.ai Holdings (NYSE:BBAI) on Thursday said it has entered into separate, privately negotiated exchange agreements with a limited number of holders of its 6.00% convertible senior notes due 2026.
  • Pursuant to such exchange agreements, BigBear.ai will exchange about $182.3 million in aggregate principal amount of the existing convertible notes for $182.3 million in aggregate principal amount of new 6.00% convertible senior secured notes due 2029 and approximately $0.4 million in cash, with such cash payment representing the accrued and unpaid interest on such Existing Convertible Notes.
  • The exchange transactions are expected to settle on or about December 27, 2024.
  • Upon completion of the exchange transactions, the aggregate principal amount of the existing convertible notes outstanding will be approximately $17.7 million.
  • BigBear.ai will not receive any cash proceeds from the issuance of the New convertible notes pursuant to the exchange transactions.
  • The new convertible notes will be senior, secured obligations of BigBear.ai and will accrue interest at a rate of 6.00% per annum, if interest is paid in cash and 7.00% per annum, if BigBear.ai elects to pay interest in kind with the shares of its common stock in each case payable semi-annually in arrears on June 15 and December 15 of each year, beginning on June 15, 2025.
  • The new convertible notes will mature on December 15, 2029, unless earlier converted, redeemed or repurchased by BigBear.ai.
  • Holders who convert their New Convertible Notes will also be entitled to an interest make-whole payment of up to 7.50% of the aggregate principal amount of notes converted.
  • The New Convertible Notes will not be redeemable at BigBear.ai’s election before December 27, 2025.
  • BBAI +18.84% premarket to $3.72.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Earnings Snapshot: Paychex beats top-line and bottom-line estimates; reaffirms FY25 outlook
iCoreConnect to effect 1-for-20 reverse stock split
data.description:
• Cloud-based software and technology solutions provider iCoreConnect (NASDAQ:ICCT) announced on Thursday the implementation of a 1-for-20 reverse stock split.
  • ICCT shares were trading -13.09% pre-market at $0.15.
  • The reverse stock split is effective 11:59 PM ET on December 20.
  • The new CUSIP number will be 450958301.
  • The number of shares outstanding will reduce from ~33M to ~1.6M.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Philly Fed outlook falls to 22-month low
data.description:
• December Philly Fed Business Outlook: -16.4 vs. +2.5 consensus, -5.5 prior.
  • The employment index +6.6 vs. +8.6.
  • New Orders -4.3 vs. prior +8.9, lowest reading since May.
  • The diffusion index for current general activity declined from -5.5 to -16.4 in December, its lowest reading since April 2023.
  • Manufacturing activity in the region declined overall, according to the firms responding to the December Manufacturing Business Outlook Survey. The survey’s indicator for current general activity remained negative and fell further, while the indexes for new orders and shipments declined and turned negative. The employment index edged down but continued to suggest increases in employment overall. The prices paid index moved higher, while the prices received index declined; both indexes continue to indicate overall increases in prices. The survey’s broad indicators for future activity continue to suggest widespread expectations for growth over the next six months.

More on economic indicators

  • Initial jobless claims fall more than expected in past week
  • U.S. GDP revised up to 3.1% in third estimate

data.title: reAlpha Tech plans to allocate up to 25% of excess cash to crypto
data.description:
• Real estate technology company reAlpha Tech (NASDAQ:AIRE) announced on Thursday a plan to allocate up to 25% of excess cash towards cryptocurrency purchases.
  • AIRE's board has approved an investment policy for the purchase of cryptocurrencies, and Bitcoin, Ethereum and Solana are set to become primary treasury reserve assets.
  • Any cash in excess of the estimated six-month period operating expenses are intended to be used to fund the purchases.
  • The company's decision to adopt cryptocurrencies as primary treasury reserve assets is a part of its strategy to diversify treasury holdings, currently comprised of only cash.
  • Source: Press Release
